3.happening: Prepend a dummy sentinel (value 0) before the real nodes, so values = [0] + input.
3.why: With a node in front of the head, deleting the real head uses the same logic as deleting any other node — no special case.
3.invariant: The dummy at index 0 is never removed.

4.happening: Build links: node i points to node i+1, and the last node points to -1 (null).
4.why: The renderer draws one arrow per link; -1 means the chain ends here.
4.invariant: links describes the current arrows between nodes.

5.happening: removed starts all zeros — no node has been deleted yet.
5.why: A 1 will flag a node so the renderer paints it red.

6.happening: changed_links starts empty — no arrow has been rerouted yet.
6.why: Each step it names the arrow that just changed so the renderer can flash it.
6.invariant: changed_links is empty unless a bypass just happened.

7.why: We always inspect current's NEXT node, never current itself.
7.invariant: current sits just before the node under inspection.

8.happening: current still has a next node, so keep scanning.
8.why: There is another candidate node to test.

8.happening: current.next is -1 (null) — the scan is finished.
8.why: No more nodes lie ahead; every node has been checked.

9.happening: Look at the candidate nxt = links[current] — the node right after current.
9.why: This is the node we'll decide to delete or keep.

10.happening: The candidate's value ({values[nxt]}) equals val ({val}) — mark it for deletion.
10.why: Every node holding val must be removed.

10.happening: The candidate's value ({values[nxt]}) is not val ({val}) — it stays.
10.why: Only nodes whose value equals val are removed.
10.invariant: current will advance past this node.

11.happening: Bypass: current's arrow now skips the candidate and points to whatever followed it.
11.why: Never following the matched node makes it unreachable — that is the deletion.
11.invariant: The live list is one node shorter.

12.happening: Detach the removed node's own arrow (set it to -1).
12.why: It should dangle off the live list, not point back into it.
12.invariant: The removed node links to nothing.

13.happening: Flag the candidate as removed — the renderer paints it red.
13.why: Removed nodes stay on screen (red) so you can see exactly what was skipped.

14.happening: Record current's rerouted arrow so it flashes this step.
14.why: The bypass is the key moment; the flash draws the eye to the new link.

16.happening: Keep the candidate: advance current onto it.
16.why: This node survives, so move forward to test the next one.
16.invariant: current moved one node to the right.

17.happening: No arrow changed this step, so clear the flash.
17.why: Nothing was rerouted; don't flash a stale arrow.

18.happening: Start collecting the surviving values into result.
18.why: The answer is the values still reachable from the dummy.

19.happening: walk starts at links[0] — the node right after the dummy, i.e. the new head.
19.why: The dummy's next is where the cleaned list really begins.
19.invariant: walk is the new head (or -1 if every node was removed).

20.happening: walk points at a surviving node — record it.
20.why: Collect every remaining node in order.

20.happening: walk is -1 — the end of the cleaned list.

21.happening: Append this surviving node's value ({values[walk]}) to result.
21.why: It wasn't removed, so it belongs in the answer.

22.happening: Follow the arrow to the next surviving node.

23.happening: Return result ({result}) — the list with every val-node removed.
23.why: This is the new head onward, the answer the problem asks for.

init.happening: Prepending the dummy sentinel and wiring the initial arrows.
init.why: The dummy lets head-deletion reuse the same pointer logic as any other node.
init.invariant: dummy(0) -> head -> ... -> -1; current sits on the dummy.

loop.happening: Checking whether current has another node ahead.
loop.why: We scan until current.next is null.
loop.invariant: When entered, current.next is a real node.

check.happening: Comparing the candidate node's value to val.
check.why: Only nodes whose value equals val are deleted.
check.invariant: The candidate is about to be bypassed or kept.

update.happening: Rewiring arrows and marking removed nodes.
update.why: Deletion is done by pointer-skipping, not by shifting data.
update.invariant: The live chain reflects every deletion so far.

move.happening: Advancing a pointer along the list.
move.why: Move forward once a node is kept, then walk the final surviving chain.
move.invariant: The pointer steps one node to the right.

return.happening: Returning the cleaned list of values.
return.why: Every val-valued node has been bypassed.
return.invariant: result holds only surviving values, in original order.
